title: A faster way to estimate AI power consumption | MIT News | Massachusetts Institute of Technology
description: The EnergAIzer technique can predict how much power a certain AI workload will consume when run on a particular processor. This method could help data center operators and algorithm developers improve the sustainability of AI workloads.

title: MIT scientists build the world’s largest collection of Olympiad-level math problems, and open it to everyone | MIT News | Massachusetts Institute of Technology
description: MIT CSAIL scientists have compiled the largest high-quality dataset of proof-based math problems ever created. It can help researchers test AI models’ mathematical reasoning, while capturing the full range of mathematical perspectives and problem-solving traditions within the global math community.

title: Teaching AI models to say “I’m not sure” | MIT News | Massachusetts Institute of Technology
description: MIT CSAIL's “Reinforcement Learning with Calibration Rewards” technique improves AI confidence estimates without sacrificing performance, addressing a root cause of hallucination in reasoning models.

title: Jacob Andreas and Brett McGuire named Edgerton Award winners | MIT News | Massachusetts Institute of Technology
description: MIT associate professors Jacob Andreas and Brett McGuire have been selected as the winners of the 2026 Harold E. Edgerton Faculty Achievement Award for exceptional contributions to teaching, research, and service at MIT.

title: Bringing AI-driven protein-design tools to biologists everywhere | MIT News | Massachusetts Institute of Technology
description: OpenProtein.AI is helping biologists stay on the cutting edge of AI with a no-code platform for protein engineering. It was founded by MIT alumni Tristan Bepler and Tim Lu.

title: Human-machine teaming dives underwater | MIT News | Massachusetts Institute of Technology
description: MIT Lincoln Laboratory researchers are developing hardware and algorithms for underwater navigation and perception to improve collaboration between divers and autonomous underwater vehicles engaged in maritime missions.

title: Q&A: MIT SHASS and the future of education in the age of AI | MIT News | Massachusetts Institute of Technology
description: As the MIT School of Humanities, Arts, and Social Sciences marks its 75th anniversary, Dean Agustín Rayo discusses why the need for developing students with broad minds and human understanding is as urgent as ever, given pressing challenges in the midst of a new technological revolution.

title: A philosophy of work | MIT News | Massachusetts Institute of Technology
description: Michal Masny, the NC Ethics of Technology Postdoctoral Fellow at MIT, is advancing dialogue, teaching, and research into the social and ethical dimensions of new computing technologies.

title: New technique makes AI models leaner and faster while they’re still learning | MIT News | Massachusetts Institute of Technology
description: CompreSSM, an algorithm from MIT CSAIL, trims dead weight from AI models, shedding unnecessary complexity while also making them faster as they continue to learn. It helps the model find its own efficient structure while cutting compute costs.

title: Sixteen new START.nano companies are developing hard-tech solutions with the support of MIT.nano | MIT News | Massachusetts Institute of Technology
description: Sixteen new companies started using MIT.nano in 2025 as participants in the START.nano program. Aimed at speeding the transition of hard-tech innovation to market, START.nano supports new ventures through discounted use of MIT.nano’s facilities and access to the MIT innovation ecosystem.

title: Helping data centers deliver higher performance with less hardware | MIT News | Massachusetts Institute of Technology
description: MIT researchers developed an intelligent system for balancing the tasks of storage devices inside a data center, which can extend the longevity of storage hardware and help a data center operate more efficiently.

title: Working to advance the nuclear renaissance | MIT News | Massachusetts Institute of Technology
description: MIT Assistant Professor Dean Price sees a bright future for nuclear power, and believes AI can help us realize that vision. AI and machine learning methods are good at finding patterns concealed within data, such as correlations between variables critical to the functioning of a nuclear plant.

title: Evaluating the ethics of autonomous systems | MIT News | Massachusetts Institute of Technology
description: SEED-SET is a new evaluation framework that can test whether recommendations of autonomous systems are well-aligned with human-defined ethical criteria. It can also pinpoint unexpected scenarios that violate ethical preferences.

title: Preview tool helps makers visualize 3D-printed objects | MIT News | Massachusetts Institute of Technology
description: The VisiPrint tool generates an accurate, aesthetics-first preview of how an object will look before it is 3D printed, in an effort to help makers avoid reprints that waste time, effort, and material.

title: MIT researchers use AI to uncover atomic defects in materials | MIT News | Massachusetts Institute of Technology
description: MIT researchers developed an AI model that can measure the types and concentrations of atomic defects used to improve materials’ strength, conductivity, and energy-conversion efficiency.

title: Seeing sounds | MIT News | Massachusetts Institute of Technology
description: Carlos Mariano Salcedo, a student in the MIT Music Technology and Computation Graduate Program, is designing AI to visualize music and other sounds.

title: MIT engineers design proteins by their motion, not just their shape | MIT News | Massachusetts Institute of Technology
description: VibeGen is a new generative AI model that designs proteins with dynamic vibration and movement. The model, developed at MIT, opens new possibilities for dynamic biomaterials and adaptive therapeutics.

title: AI system learns to keep warehouse robot traffic running smoothly | MIT News | Massachusetts Institute of Technology
description: A new system increases throughput in automated warehouses by adaptively determining which robots should go first to avoid congestion and collisions. The work was led by researchers from MIT and Symbotic.

title: Augmenting citizen science with computer vision for fish monitoring | MIT News | Massachusetts Institute of Technology
description: Monitoring river herring migration is essential for conservation and fisheries management. Researchers have now demonstrated a method using video and computer vision to supplement traditional methods and volunteer-based programs.

title: Wristband enables wearers to control a robotic hand with their own movements | MIT News | Massachusetts Institute of Technology
description: MIT engineers designed an ultrasound wristband that precisely tracks a wearer’s hand movements in real time and communicates the information to a robot or a virtual environment.

title: How to create “humble” AI | MIT News | Massachusetts Institute of Technology
description: An MIT-led team developed a framework for creating “humble” AI systems that reveal when they are not confident in their medical diagnoses or recommendations, and encourage users to gather additional information when the diagnosis is uncertain.

title: Advancing international trade research and finding community | MIT News | Massachusetts Institute of Technology
description: When Sojun Park came across a public posting about the Postdoctoral Associate Program at the MIT Center for International Studies, he took a chance and applied.

title: On algorithms, life, and learning | MIT News | Massachusetts Institute of Technology
description: MIT Professor Dimitris Bertsimas, an operations research expert, delivered the 54th James R. Killian Faculty Achievement Award Lecture, describing his past and current work.

title: What’s the right path for AI? | MIT News | Massachusetts Institute of Technology
description: Speakers and audience members grappled with the many dimensions of AI’s impact at a conference at MIT Wednesday. Journalist Karen Hao ’15 and scholar Paola Ricaurte delivered the keynotes.

title: MIT and Hasso Plattner Institute establish collaborative hub for AI and creativity | MIT News | Massachusetts Institute of Technology
description: MIT and the Hasso Plattner Institute establish the MIT and HPI AI and Creativity Hub, a 10-year collaboration led by the MIT School of Architecture and Planning, MIT Schwarzman College of Computing, and MIT Morningside Academy for Design to advance research and education at the intersection of AI, design, and creativity.

title: Generative AI improves a wireless vision system that sees through obstructions | MIT News | Massachusetts Institute of Technology
description: Wave-Former is a new system that can complete the shape of a hidden 3D object or reconstruct the scene of an entire interior room using reflected wireless signals.

title: A better method for identifying overconfident large language models | MIT News | Massachusetts Institute of Technology
description: A new technique for assessing the reliability of a large language model’s predictions is better at identifying when the model is confident, but wrong. This more accurate method for uncertainty quantification could help users know whether to trust the model’s outputs.

title: Sustaining diplomacy amid competition in US-China relations | MIT News | Massachusetts Institute of Technology
description: At an MIT Energy Initiative event, Nicholas Burns, former U.S. ambassador to China, highlighted climate change as an area for diplomatic engagement between the two nations, while exploring areas of competition, including China's greater emphasis on STEM education.

title: MIT-IBM Watson AI Lab seed to signal: Amplifying early-career faculty impact | MIT News | Massachusetts Institute of Technology
description: Five MIT faculty explain how early engagement with the MIT-IBM Watson AI Lab, an academic-industry collaboration, has positively impacted their careers, growth of their research groups, areas of AI focus, and successes.

title: Can AI help predict which heart-failure patients will worsen within a year? | MIT News | Massachusetts Institute of Technology
description: A new deep learning model can predict a patient's heart failure trajectory up to a year in advance. The work was developed by researchers at MIT, Mass General Brigham, and Harvard Medical School.

title: 3 Questions: On the future of AI and the mathematical and physical sciences | MIT News | Massachusetts Institute of Technology
description: MIT Professor Jesse Thaler discusses the evolving relationship between artificial intelligence and the mathematical and physical sciences.

title: New MIT class uses anthropology to improve chatbots | MIT News | Massachusetts Institute of Technology
description: MIT’s new interdisciplinary class "Humane User Experience Design" blends anthropology, human-computer interaction, and UX design to teach students how to create humane, culturally informed AI chatbots that improve social engagement, ethical interaction, and real-world conversational experiences.

title: A better method for planning complex visual tasks | MIT News | Massachusetts Institute of Technology
description: A new AI-driven system generates plans for long-term, complex tasks about twice as well as some existing methods. The technique, developed at MIT, uses two vision-language models that work together to simulate actions and produce files for formal planning software.

title: 3 Questions: Building predictive models to characterize tumor progression | MIT News | Massachusetts Institute of Technology
description: MIT Assistant Professor Matthew G. Jones is working to decode molecular processes on the genetic, epigenetic, and microenvironment levels to anticipate how and when tumors evolve to resist treatment.

title: How Joseph Paradiso’s sensing innovations bridge the arts, medicine, and ecology | MIT News | Massachusetts Institute of Technology
description: MIT Media Lab professor Joseph Paradiso pioneers wearable and environmental sensing technologies, bridging art, medicine, and ecology. His interdisciplinary research transforms motion, health, and ecological data into real-world applications that connect people and understand the world.

title: Improving AI models’ ability to explain their predictions | MIT News | Massachusetts Institute of Technology
description: A new technique transforms any computer vision model into one that can explain its predictions using a set of concepts a human could understand. The method generates more appropriate concepts that boost the accuracy of the model.

title: A “ChatGPT for spreadsheets” helps solve difficult engineering challenges faster | MIT News | Massachusetts Institute of Technology
description: MIT researchers developed a computational approach that can be used to solve problems with hundreds of variables. In tests on realistic engineering challenges, the approach found top solutions 10 to 100 times faster than methods such as Bayesian optimization.

title: Featured video: Coding for underwater robotics | MIT News | Massachusetts Institute of Technology
description: Drawn by the chance to tackle new problems and cutting-edge algorithm development, Ivy Mahncke undertook an internship with MIT Lincoln Laboratory, developing an algorithm that would help a human diver and robotic vehicle navigate underwater.

title: New method could increase LLM training efficiency | MIT News | Massachusetts Institute of Technology
description: A new technique leverages computing downtime to accelerate the training process for reasoning large language models (LLMs) without any additional computational overhead.

title: Mixing generative AI with physics to create personal items that work in the real world | MIT News | Massachusetts Institute of Technology
description: A system known as PhysiOpt complements generative AI models with physics simulations to create 3D models of personal items. Users can prompt the system using text or images, and get a blueprint that works in the real world when fabricated.

title: AI to help researchers see the bigger picture in cell biology | MIT News | Massachusetts Institute of Technology
description: A new AI framework identifies which data about a cell are captured by one measurement modality and which are shared across multiple modalities. This gives researchers a more complete picture of the cell state and could help them understand disease mechanisms and plan treatments.

title: Study: AI chatbots provide less-accurate information to vulnerable users | MIT News | Massachusetts Institute of Technology
description: MIT researchers find AI chatbots often show bias, giving less accurate or more dismissive answers to some users. The findings highlight growing risks, especially for marginalized communities worldwide.

title: Exposing biases, moods, personalities, and abstract concepts hidden in large language models | MIT News | Massachusetts Institute of Technology
description: A new method can test whether a large language model contains hidden biases, personalities, moods, or other abstract concepts. MIT researchers can zero in on connections within a model that encode for a concept of interest, to improve LLM safety and performance.

title: Parking-aware navigation system could prevent frustration and emissions | MIT News | Massachusetts Institute of Technology
description: By minimizing the need to drive around looking for a parking spot, this technique can save drivers up to 35 minutes — and give them a realistic estimate of total travel time.

title: Personalization features can make LLMs more agreeable | MIT News | Massachusetts Institute of Technology
description: User context in long-term interaction data increases the likelihood an LLM will become overly agreeable or begin to mirror the user’s viewpoints. This phenomenon, known as sycophancy, can harm a model’s accuracy or create an echo chamber that can proliferate misinformation.

title: New J-PAL research and policy initiative to test and scale AI innovations to fight poverty | MIT News | Massachusetts Institute of Technology
description: Project AI Evidence, a new J-PAL research and policy initiative, will fund impact evaluations of AI innovations in the fight against poverty and scale up those that are most successful, in collaboration with governments, tech companies, NGOs, and researchers.

title: Accelerating science with AI and simulations | MIT News | Massachusetts Institute of Technology
description: For more than a decade, MIT Associate Professor Rafael Gómez-Bombarelli has used artificial intelligence to create new materials. Now, he believes AI is poised to transform science in ways never before possible.

title: 3 Questions: Using AI to help Olympic skaters land a quint | MIT News | Massachusetts Institute of Technology
description: MIT Sports Lab researchers discuss applying AI to sports such as figure skating, whether AI systems could ever be used to judge Olympic events, and when we might see a skater land a quint.

title: Study: Platforms that rank the latest LLMs can be unreliable | MIT News | Massachusetts Institute of Technology
description: The results of popular LLM ranking platforms can be skewed by just a few data points, possibly providing an unreliable report about which LLM would perform best in real situations, according MIT researchers who also developed a way to test the rankings and identify these influential data points.

title: “This is science!” – MIT president talks about the importance of America’s research enterprise on GBH’s Boston Public Radio | MIT News | Massachusetts Institute of Technology
description: MIT faculty join The Curiosity Desk to discuss football, math, Olympic figure skating, AI and the quest to cure ovarian cancer.
